feed.title: SAP Community - SD (Sales and Distribution)
feed.subtitle: SD (Sales and Distribution) Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: AATP Product Allocation table in Fiscal Period instead of Calendar Month
entry.content:
Hi Gurus 

I hope you can help me with an answer or point me to a source where I might find it ...

When it comes to time dimension, all our Supply Planning (including Demand Planning) is done in Fiscal Periods which don't match the calendar month.

Currently the set up of the Product Allocation table uses calendar month. It would be more convenient to use Fiscal month. That would match the data in IBP (we use SAP IBP for Demand Planning and Supply Planning), easier for the demand planner and easier for the integration IBP (via CI-DS) to S4. Is that possible, to use a different time unit? Does it has to be necessarily calendar month?

Thanks in advance

Best regards

Roberto

entry.title: The difference between tax categories TTX1 and MWST.
entry.content:
Hello,

Regarding the tax determination of sales order, the default tax category has changed from MWST to TTX1, but what are the differences between these two tax categories?

entry.title: VK11 automatic calculation of an alternative unit of measure
entry.content: Hi colleagues, in transaction vk11 user can create separately manually sales price for item per piece and per package C0*, for all alternative units of measurement that are entered for this item in master data. But it is necessary to make an automatic calculation of the price per alternative unit of measure when the price per piece is saved. This should happen exactly in the process of price creation. I can"t find a standard SAP solution. Perhaps this should be done via a formula (TA VOFM) and add this formula to the access sequence. But maybe there are other options that I would like to ask you about. Thank you in advance

entry.title: Route change in sales order
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have a scenario, in which we have a sales order item with route (Road), later if we change the route, will this impact the sales order confirmation dates in standard?

entry.title: MD04 Available qty not correct as per scope of check
entry.content:
Hi, 

In MD04, the "Available stock" is not equal to the ATP Scope of check, instead it's combining all the stock type quantities from MMBE and showing it as Available stock in MD04. Even when I run the back-order process, it is still not correct (all stock types showing available)

I have defined the scope of check to exclude Quality stock, exclude Vendor sub con stock and Exclude Blocked stock as well. But all those three stock types are showing at "Available stock " in MD04.

I know the to disable to block stock at plant level in OPPI, that's still enabled, we don't to enable that at plant level. Besides that the Quality stock and Sun con stock should not show as available stock.

Also based on what Availability checking rule does the MD04 shows the stock? All of my checking rule are without special stock. 

OPJJ= Scope of check PP maintained to exclude quality and blocked stock.

entry.title: Cancel goods issue as material has not reached the customer
entry.content:
Hi All,

How can we cancel the goods issue, if we do a goods issue and find out that there is something wrong with the package, but material is still in out truck and has not reached the customer.

Do we still need to create a return PO or there is any process to cancel the goods issue and invoices in standard SAP?

entry.title: (PT) ATCUD and Digital signature in SOAP API
entry.content:
Hello,

For sending billing documents in Portugal market, we are considering using SOAP API “CO_EDI_INVOICE_REQUEST_OUT” in S/4 (https://api.sap.com/api/CO_EDI_INVOICE_REQUEST_OUT/resource).

In Portugal it is mandatory to communicate the Digital Signature information with the billing document, in the SOAP API it is done with the following segments:

 

  • PT_DigitalSignature - optional; type PT_DigitalSignature
    • PT_DocumentNo - optional; type PT_DocumentNo - type string with restriction maxLength(20)
    • PT_QRCode - optional; type PT_QRCode - type string with restriction
    • PT_SgntrCertificate - optional; type PT_SgntrCertificate - type string with restriction maxLength(20)
    • PT_SeriesDocumentNumber - optional; type PT_SeriesDocumentNumber - type string with restriction maxLength(70)
    • PT_HashKey - optional; type PT_HashKey - type string with restriction maxLength(7)
    •  

But we must be on the wrong version of SOAP API “CO_EDI_INVOICE_REQUEST_OUT” in S/4, because these segments are not part of the API for us. So now to the question, does anyone know the class methods and/or function modules we can use to obtain this segment information (PT_DigitalSignature+sub elements) information and attach it to the API message? (We have checked the overall note 3154599 and there is nothing relevant for us..).

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Subcontracting providing components to the vendor (SD > MM)
entry.content:
have a scenario where:

When a customer requests a sales material X, I provide components X1 and X2 to a subcontractor, who then completes material X and delivers it directly.

How would you resolve this?

I believe the ideal approach is:

  • Creation of a Sales Order with material X.
  • Automatic creation of a Purchase Requisition (PR) with the subcontractor vendor determined automatically.
  • Automatic creation of a Purchase Order (PO) (using RM06BB30) from the PR with the BOM of material X exploded.

Have you implemented something like this? Do you think this is the most appropriate approach? 

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Error to create sales order (error 8B 213)
entry.content:
Our company is trying to place an intercompany Sales Order for Brazil Sales Org. against an US plant. The system is returning an error related to Business Plant and Plant. 

Business place not found for company XXXX plant XXXX

Message no. 8B213

Other company codes are already working with this format of Sales order. The problem occur only for Brazil. At the configuration in SAP for Plant and Business Place there is no option to include a new entry. In configuration the only option is to change the entries that are already there.

entry.title: Empty batch in vl10c->background
entry.content:
Hi, experts why i'm getting an emty batch, when i'm creating a delivery with vl10c->background from sales order.
When i go with va02->Sales Document-> Deliver everything good.
also it's good with vl10c->dialog

entry.title: Standard Prepayment process in SAP ERP 6.0
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have completed the functional configuration for the standard prepayment process. However, I am not confident about how the SD pricing condition types AZWA, AZWB, and AZDI are functioning. Could someone please clarify how these conditions are expected to behave in the sales order, pro forma/down payment invoice, and final invoice?

For example:- I created a sales order and saved it, but noticed that the pricing condition types AZWA, AZWB, and AZDI are appearing with zero value. I then tried to create a pro forma/down payment invoice (FAZ), and again observed that these condition types are showing as zero. I received the payment using transaction code F-29 for the above pro forma/down payment invoice.

Subsequently, I proceeded to create the delivery and final invoice (F2), but once more, the pricing condition types AZWA, AZWB, and AZDI were displayed with zero value. attached the screenshot for reference.

Could someone kindly share their expertise and provide guidance on this process? Am I missing something or doing anything incorrectly?

Best regards,

Subhramanyu

entry.title: Configuration of Multiple VAT Rates in Sales Orders in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
I am a junior functional consultant for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Sales and Distribution. Another consultant has asked me to investigate a tax-related topic within Sales Orders in S/4HANA, and I’m still becoming familiar with the system. Within the sales order, in the tax breakdown section, is it possible to apply two VAT rates at the same time, for example 8% and 16%? Can this be configured based on location?
Is it possible to enter taxes manually, or is it fully dependent on the Business Partner master data? I’m based in Mexico. Thank you for your support!

entry.title: if the next order is processed in VF01 SAP, an invoice without items is created.
entry.content:
Hello, everybody.

S4Hana

In transaction VF01 I process the first order - invoice is created, no errors. Then, without leaving the VF01 transaction, I specify the next order, but two invoices are created during processing. The first one is correct and corresponds to the order, the second one is erroneous with empty data, without item lines. The time of creation is identical in the two invoices. If I continue to process new orders, the invoices are created three, four, etc. The first invoice in the set is correct, the other invoices are empty with no line items.

I understand that this is a program error, not settings, because there is a parallel ERP system (not S4Hana) and there is no such error, and the settings are the same. I can't find a note to fix the error.

entry.title: Slow update - RSM13000 (SAPMV45A/SAPMV60A/SAPMV75A/SAPMV56A...)
entry.content:
Hello guys,

in our system like 30% of V* transactions have slow update.
Example: VA01, VA02, VA22, VT02n, VT03n, VF11…

During this slow update, users are waiting and complaining.

In ST03n I can see, 
Update V1 = slow
Update V2 = fast

There is no bottleneck in Workprocesses (UPD*) or CPU usage or anything else.
There are also several instances where update request gets distributed.
Also no problem with hanging HTTP RFC connections (SAPLHTTP_RUNTIME...).
As far as I can see there is no locking problem in SM12.

Most of time is always used under "Processing Time".

The only thing I can see is high "RFC idle time"... can this be a reason (?)
Because "calling time" and "data send time" are pretty low.


ARCHIV_CREATE_TABLE_MULT


The UPD WP is remaining in program SAPLCEI3 without more information, only time counts...
and after 5-20 seconds it will finish.

 

This happens with ~30% of update requests in V* transactions.

Maybe you have some other ideas what to check?

Thank you.

entry.title: Free of charge samples sales for Brazil with NFE
entry.content:
Hi all,

We have a new requirement from our Brazil client to implement Free of charge scenario.

We have implemented a custom solution already, but the customer master needs to be set up in a particular way so that the system can determine the tax values correctly. 

Now they are looking for a free of charge sales which can be applied to all customers. It should have a NFE generated, and net value = zero, but ICMS should be determined.

Could you please guide me on this? Is there a configuration setup which is provided in standard SAP which I can use?

Thanks,
Vishnu

entry.title: Customer Master Data - Extension
entry.content:
Hi,

For customer extension, it is mandatory that the customer master data already exists in the system — this means you can only perform the extension after the customer master has been successfully uploaded.

Separate Template for Extension:
A dedicated template is used for customer master data extension — this is different from the normal creation template, given below :

  • Customer - extend existing record by new org levels

(Here we are talking with the scenario)

Run ID is Critical:
A unique Run ID is assigned for each extension. If Customer A is being extended to 2 sales areas, then two separate Run IDs will be used, e.g.:

  • Sales Area 1: Run ID = 1

  • Sales Area 2: Run ID = 2

Sequential Processing Required:
Extension uploads must be done sequentially:
First, the uploader for Run ID 1 will be executed (for Sales Area 1),
Then, the uploader for Run ID 2 will be executed (for Sales Area 2).

Separate Uploaders for Each Run ID:
Multiple sales area extensions are not allowed in a single uploader. The system does not accept so many changes in one uploader, therefore, each Run ID must be processed in a separate uploader.

Customer Must Exist in the System:
Before performing any extension, it is mandatory that the customer master data already exists in the system. This means the extension can only be carried out after the initial customer creation/upload has been successfully completed.

Best regards,

Tehreem Fatima

entry.title: Ship to party
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP Public cloud; There are multiple Ship-To Parties for a single customer how can we upload them without maintaining manually, one by one, against each Sold-To Party?

To explain further: let's say Customer A has 20 Ship-To Parties. How can we upload them efficiently and save time? I’ve already uploaded 100+ Ship-To Parties into the system, but now assigning each one individually to the corresponding Sold-To Party feels like a very time-consuming task.

Is there no shortcut or quicker method to assign these Ship-To Parties in bulk?

Best regards,

entry.title: Looking to Understand User Exits in SAP SD with Real-Time Case Studies
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I’m currently working in the SAP SD module and looking to expand my knowledge in the area of User Exits, specifically within the Sales and Distribution (SD) domain.

I would like to mention that I am completely new to this functionality and eager to understand it from the ground up. I’ve come across the term "User Exit" quite often in discussions related to customization and enhancements, but I’m still unclear about:

  • What exactly are User Exits and how do they differ from BADIs or Enhancements?

  • Where and how are they implemented in SAP SD?

  • What are some real-time business use cases where User Exits have been effectively used to solve specific problems or meet business requirements?

If possible, I would greatly appreciate step-by-step explanations or references to detailed examples that can help a beginner like me understand this concept in depth. Any inputs, documents, or links would be very helpful!

Thanks in advance for your time and guidance.

Best regards,
Sobhagya Thapa

entry.title: LIKP-TM_WHSE_EXEC value is not getting updated with the value 03 when outbound delivery saved in S4
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a decentralized WM system integrated with SAP S4 HANA, As soon as outbound delivery created the delivery data will be distributed through an IDOC via output type and the delivery distribution flag(LIKP-VLSTK) should be set to B(distributed) from D(Planned for Distribution)  and warehouse execution status( LIKP-TM_WHSE_EXEC) should be set to '03'(Delivery is in Warehouse Processing). 

we are using SAP standard FM: SHP_CHG_DLV_DISTRB_STATE in the output type driver program to set the delivery distribution flag(LIKP-VLSTK)  from D(Planned for Distribution) to B(distributed) which is happening as expected.

As per standard behavior the warehouse execution status( LIKP-TM_WHSE_EXEC) should be set to '03'(Delivery is in Warehouse Processing) as soon as LIKP-VLSTK value set to B. But  it is not happening .

Seeking your advice if anything is missing from config side or any other suggestions ?

entry.title: Hi, We are also facing the same issue. has anyone found the solution to this issue?
